policy:
Updated agent operational policy (complete). Rules are concrete, testable, and based only on behaviors shown in the trajectories. Where a behavior was seen only occasionally and is not clearly mandatory, it is labeled "Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory)".

1. Information Gathering Rules
- IF the user requests any account- or order-related action (returns, exchanges, modifications, address change, cancellations, order-status lookup, reorder, count of product options, order lookup) → THEN ask for first_name, last_name, and zip before any account lookup, unless the user immediately supplies an email.
- IF the assistant asks for first_name, last_name, and zip AND the user hesitates about providing zip or questions why zip is needed → THEN offer the alternative of providing an email instead.
- IF the user supplies first_name, last_name, and zip (in one reply) → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip) immediately.
- IF the user supplies an email at any time (alone or together with name/zip) → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email) immediately, and do not wait to call find_user_id_by_name_zip.
- IF find_user_id_by_name_zip(...) returns Error: User not found → THEN ask the user for email immediately and call find_user_id_by_email(email) immediately after receiving it.
- IF the user explicitly refuses to provide first_name/last_name/zip OR asks to look up by order id or email instead → THEN ask for email and call find_user_id_by_email(email) immediately.
- IF the user supplies multiple candidate emails → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email) on each candidate email in the order supplied until one returns a user_id.
- IF find_user_id_by_name_zip OR find_user_id_by_email returns a user_id → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) immediately.
- IF get_user_details returns user.orders and the user asks the assistant to locate an item/order (including requests like "help with a few recent orders", "I got two tablets", "modify the grill I just ordered", or "all my pending orders") → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) on one or more order ids from get_user_details(user_id) to find the relevant order(s) and items. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory.)
- IF the user references "the one in <city/state>" or references another order as the source address → THEN call get_order_details on the referenced order id to extract its address fields for use in modify_pending_order_address or modify_user_address.
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): assistant sometimes prompts for first_name/last_name/zip even when the user's request is only product information (no account/change needed); when the user provides these, the assistant proceeds to find the account.

2. Selection Rules (product/variant mapping and choosing)
- IF the assistant needs to map a product name given by the user to a product_id → THEN call list_all_product_types() and use its mapping to obtain the product_id before any call to get_product_details(product_id).
- IF the assistant has a product_id and needs variant-level details → THEN call get_product_details(product_id) and use the returned variants for selection.
- IF the user provides a desired new variant by specifying exact option attributes (color, size, material, style, etc.) → THEN from get_product_details(product_id) select the variant whose options exactly match the requested attributes AND whose "available" flag == true; use that variant's item_id as new_item_id.
- IF the user requests to change only one attribute and specifies only that attribute → THEN from get_product_details(product_id) select the variant whose options equal the original item's options except for that one attribute AND whose "available" flag == true; use that variant's item_id as new_item_id.
- IF the user requests the "cheapest" available version of a product WITH an attribute constraint (e.g., "cheapest green") → THEN from get_product_details(product_id) filter variants to those whose option for that attribute equals the requested value AND available==true; pick the variant among that set with minimum price; use that variant's item_id.
- IF the user requests the cheapest model with processor "i7 or better" → THEN from get_product_details(product_id) filter variants to options.processor in {"i7","i9"}; among available==true choose the variant with minimum price; use that variant's item_id.
- IF the user requests an exchange keeping all attributes the same except one (e.g., "highest zoom") → THEN call get_product_details(product_id); filter variants to those matching the original item's options for every attribute except the attribute to change; from that set choose the variant with numerically largest value for that attribute where available==true; if multiple tie, pick any one.
- IF the user provides a specific item_id (variant id) as the target (for example: "change it to item number 9844888101") → THEN:
  - call list_all_product_types() if needed to locate product_id (when only product name was initially given),
  - call get_product_details(product_id) to validate the provided item_id exists among variants,
  - IF the provided item_id is present and variant.available == true → THEN use the provided item_id as new_item_id,
  - IF provided item_id is not present or available==false → THEN propose available alternatives (variants with available==true) and ask whether the user wants one. (Observed Behavior — supported by trajectories.)
- IF an exact requested-attribute variant is not available → THEN propose alternative available variants and ask whether the user wants one. (Observed Behavior — seen.)
- IF the order contains multiple items with the same product name and the user issues a request affecting "the" product (ambiguous) → THEN ask the user to specify which item (distinguish by item attributes or item_id) before making any selection or calling state-changing tools.
- IF the assistant must compute price differences or refunds for exchanges/cheapest selections or batch modifications → THEN:
  - call list_all_product_types to locate product_id (if product name was given),
  - call get_product_details(product_id) to obtain variant prices and availability,
  - compute the sum of selected variant prices (use variants with available==true) and compare to the sum of old item prices,
  - present the computed price/payment or refund amount to the user and ask for confirmation before executing the action.

3. Action Execution Rules
- modify_pending_order_items:
  - IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requests to change item(s) options AND the required new_item_id(s) are known → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids=[old_item_id(s)], new_item_ids=[new_item_id(s)], payment_method_id=<payment method id>).
  - IF new item price > old item price → THEN include a "payment" transaction charged to payment_method_id for the difference in the resulting order/payment_history.
  - IF new item price < old item price → THEN include a "refund" transaction to payment_method_id for the difference in the resulting order/payment_history.
  - IF multiple pending orders must be updated → THEN call modify_pending_order_items separately for each pending order that must be changed.
  - IF changing a pending order item requires choosing a variant → THEN follow Selection Rules (list_all_product_types -> get_product_details -> select variant).
  - IF the order contains only the item the user asked to cancel and it's pending → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) to cancel that pending order instead of modify_pending_order_items.
  - IF the user explicitly says "put it on the same card I used for the original order" for a pending modification → THEN use the payment_method_id from the order.payment_history entry as the payment_method_id for modify_pending_order_items.
  - IF the user supplies a new_item_id directly → THEN validate it via get_product_details(product_id) and, if valid and available, use it in modify_pending_order_items without additional variant selection steps.
  - IF modify_pending_order_items returns Error: item not found or Error indicating invalid item_id(s) → THEN (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory) retry the call after cleaning/stripping incidental prefixes or UI text from the supplied item_ids (for example remove "0. Cancel and go back" prefixes) and call modify_pending_order_items again with corrected item_ids.
- modify_pending_order_address:
  - IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requests to change shipping address for that order → THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip).
  - IF the user asks to update the shipping address for multiple pending orders → THEN call modify_pending_order_address separately once per pending order requested.
  - IF the user supplies "use the address from order #X" → THEN call get_order_details(order_id="#X") to extract that order.address fields and use those values in modify_pending_order_address calls for the pending orders the user requested to update.
  - AFTER modifying the requested pending order addresses AND the user explicitly also asked to update their account address (for example "set that as my default too") → THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) with the same address fields.
  - IF modify_pending_order_address returns an Error indicating unexpected keyword (e.g., 'ZIP') or malformed parameter name → THEN retry modify_pending_order_address with the parameter name 'zip' (lowercase).
- modify_user_address:
  - IF the user requests updating their account address → THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip).
  - IF the user asks to set their default shipping address to the address used on a particular order AND that order's details were retrieved earlier in the conversation → THEN use that order.address fields as the parameters to modify_user_address(user_id, ...).
  - IF a modify_user_address call returns Error: User not found due to a malformed user_id → THEN retry modify_user_address with the user_id exactly as returned by get_user_details.
  - AFTER a successful modify_user_address call (tool returns updated user object) → THEN report to the user that the account address has been modified to the supplied address.
- cancel_pending_order:
  - IF the user requests to cancel pending order(s) → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) once per pending order to be cancelled.
  - IF the user provided a cancellation reason → THEN pass that reason to cancel_pending_order.
  - IF the user does NOT provide a cancellation reason → THEN use reason="no longer needed" when cancelling. (Observed Behavior — seen repeatedly.)
  - IF the assistant explicitly asks the user "what should be the reason to cancel?" and the user supplies a reason → THEN pass that supplied reason to cancel_pending_order. (Observed Behavior — usual behavior.)
  - Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): in at least one trajectory the assistant asked for a reason but the tool call used reason="no longer needed" instead of the user's supplied reason (assistant may sometimes override provided reason with the default).
  - IF the user requests cancelling only one item in a pending order that contains multiple items → THEN inform the user that individual-item cancellation is not possible AND offer alternatives: propose modifying/swapping that item for a different variant or cancelling the entire pending order.
- return_delivered_order_items:
  - IF order.status == "delivered" AND the user requests to return delivered items from that order → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids=[...], payment_method_id=<payment method id>).
  - IF the user requests refund to "original payment" and the order has a payment_history entry for the original payment → THEN use the payment_method_id from that order.payment_history payment entry in the return_delivered_order_items call.
  - IF return_delivered_order_items returns Error: Payment method not found → THEN retry return_delivered_order_items using a valid payment_method_id available in get_user_details(payment_methods) (retry once).
  - IF the user requests returning the same product across multiple delivered orders → THEN call return_delivered_order_items separately for each delivered order that contains that item.
  - IF the assistant must compute the refund total across multiple return calls → THEN compute sum of item prices being returned (use the items' listed prices) and present the total to the user.
  - IF the user requests expected timing for refunds and asks before processing → THEN present the expected timing when presenting the computed refund amount, and ask for confirmation before calling return_delivered_order_items. (Observed Behavior — assistant asked for payment method before processing in example.)
  - IF the user requests returning all items in a delivered order ("return everything from that order") → THEN call return_delivered_order_items with the list of all item_ids from get_order_details(order_id) and use payment_method_id according to Payment Method Selection rules (see below).
- exchange_delivered_order_items:
  - IF order.status == "delivered" AND the user requests an exchange for delivered items → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids=[old_item_id(s)], new_item_ids=[new_item_id(s)], payment_method_id=<payment method id>).
  - IF multiple items from the same delivered order are being exchanged at once → THEN include all old item_ids and corresponding new_item_ids in a single exchange_delivered_order_items call for that order.
  - IF exchange_delivered_order_items returns exchange_price_difference > 0 → THEN a payment transaction is recorded against the provided payment_method_id; IF < 0 → THEN a refund transaction is recorded to that payment_method_id.
  - IF the user requests exchanged variant selection (attributes specified) → THEN follow Selection Rules to pick new_item_id from get_product_details(product_id) and present price difference before executing exchange.
  - IF the assistant needs to process exchanges for the same product across multiple delivered orders → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items separately for each order.
  - IF the selected new_item_id equals the old_item_id (same variant/options) and available==true → THEN exchange_delivered_order_items may be called with new_item_ids equal to the original item_id, producing exchange_price_difference == 0.0.
  - IF the order item being exchanged (old_item_id) is present in the order returned by get_order_details but get_product_details shows that old_item_id variant.available == false → THEN still use that old_item_id from the order as the old_item_id in exchange_delivered_order_items (old_item_id need not be currently available); validate new_item_id availability only for new_item selection.

- Payment method selection for modify/return/exchange:
  - IF the action requires a payment_method_id AND the user explicitly specified a payment_method_id string (tool id) → THEN pass that specified payment_method_id to the tool call.
  - IF the action requires a payment_method_id AND the user explicitly requested a payment_method by brand/type (for example: "Visa", "Mastercard", "PayPal") AND get_user_details(payment_methods) contains one or more methods whose brand/source matches the requested brand/type → THEN select a matching payment_method_id from get_user_details(payment_methods) whose brand/source matches and use it automatically.
  - IF the action requires a payment_method_id AND get_user_details(payment_methods) shows only one payment method on file → THEN use that single payment method automatically without asking the user.
  - IF the action requires a payment_method_id AND the user has multiple payment methods on file AND the user has not specified one → THEN ask the user which payment method to use before calling the tool.
  - IF the user asks for refund to "original payment" → THEN use payment_method_id from order.payment_history payment entry (if present) as the payment_method_id in return_delivered_order_items/exchange_delivered_order_items.
  - IF a tool call that uses a provided payment_method_id returns Error: Payment method not found → THEN retry the action using a payment_method_id present in get_user_details(payment_methods). Retry once with a valid id from get_user_details.
  - IF the user requests a payment method type that does not exist on the user's account (e.g., user asks to use a credit card but get_user_details(payment_methods) shows only a gift_card) → THEN inform the user that the requested payment method is not on file and offer transfer to a human agent OR offer available alternatives.
  - IF the user specifies a payment method but then changes their mind before the assistant executes the tool call → THEN use the most recent payment method the user specified when executing the tool call.
  - IF the original payment method for the delivered order is a gift_card → THEN refunds for that order must be to that gift_card or to another existing gift_card on the user's account; the assistant will use a gift card id from get_user_details(payment_methods) or from the order.payment_history entry.
  - IF the user says "use the other card" or similar and the order.payment_history contains a payment_method_id → THEN select a payment_method_id from get_user_details(payment_methods) whose id != order.payment_history.payment_method_id; if multiple such methods exist, choose any one. (Observed Behavior — supported by trajectories.)
  - IF the user requests refund to a payment method that is disallowed by rules (for example: refund to credit card when original payment was gift_card for a delivered order) → THEN inform the user that refunds must go to the original allowed method (original payment or gift card constraints) and offer transfer to a human agent if the user asks for escalation. (Observed Behavior — seen: assistant refused credit card refund when original payment was gift_card and informed user.)

4. Confirmation Rules
- IF recommending a specific variant or change (selecting a matching variant, the cheapest option, or highest-zoom option) → THEN present the selected variant (item_id and key options) and the price difference/refund to the user and ask whether they want that variant or how to process any refund/payment BEFORE executing the modify/return/exchange call. (Seen repeatedly.)
- IF a required parameter for the requested action is missing or ambiguous (which item to return, which payment method, whether to update all pending orders and account address) → THEN ask the user a clarifying question and wait for user confirmation/specification before making any tool call that changes state.
- IF the assistant computes a refund or payment total (returns, exchanges, batch modifications) → THEN present the computed amount and, if relevant, expected timing and ask the user to confirm before performing the call.
- IF the user explicitly confirms the suggested variant(s) or specifies the missing parameter(s) (for example: "Yes, go ahead", "Use my gift card", "Refund to original payment") → THEN perform the corresponding modify/return/exchange/modify_address/cancel tool call without additional confirmation.
- IF the user says they will "use my credit card" for payment AND get_user_details shows a single credit card → THEN proceed with charges using that card without asking for card details.
- IF the user specifies a brand/type (e.g., "Visa") and a matching card is found in get_user_details(payment_methods) AND the assistant selects that card automatically → THEN the assistant does not need an additional confirmation about which card to use.
- IF the user asks for projected balances (for example "what will my gift card balance be after this") BEFORE execution → THEN compute the projected balance using get_user_details(payment_methods).gift_card.balance minus the projected extra payments, present the projection to the user, and ask for confirmation before executing the modify/charge action. The assistant may compute and state the projected balance prior to making the tool call; execution requires user confirmation.

5. Tool Usage Constraints (call parameters, prefixes, sequencing)
- find_user_id_by_name_zip requires first_name, last_name, zip and is called immediately after receiving those three values.
- find_user_id_by_email requires an email and is called when the assistant does not find a user by name+zip or when the user supplies email.
- IF the user supplies an email along with name/zip → THEN prefer calling find_user_id_by_email(email) immediately.
- get_user_details requires a user_id returned by find_user_id_by_name_zip or find_user_id_by_email and is always called immediately after receiving a user_id.
- get_order_details requires order_id prefixed with "#" (assistant always supplies order_id beginning with "#" when calling get_order_details).
  - IF the user provides an order id without "#" (e.g., "W4284542") → THEN the assistant prefixes "#" and calls get_order_details("#W4284542").
- IF get_order_details returns Error: Order not found → THEN prompt the user to re-check the order id and retry when corrected.
- IF get_order_details was called with "#<digits>" and returned Error: Order not found → THEN assistant may retry with a "#W<digits>" prefix and call get_order_details again. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory.)
- list_all_product_types is used whenever the assistant needs to map a product name to a product_id; it must be called before get_product_details(product_id) when mapping from a product name.
- get_product_details(product_id) must be called to inspect variant-level options and availability before selecting variants or computing price differences.
- modify_pending_order_items, return_delivered_order_items, exchange_delivered_order_items require a valid payment_method_id from get_user_details(payment_methods); IF an invalid payment_method_id is passed and the tool returns a payment-method error → THEN retry using a valid payment_method_id from get_user_details.
- modify_pending_order_address must be called separately for each pending order the user asked to update.
- cancel_pending_order must be called separately for each pending order to be cancelled.
- IF the assistant receives multiple candidate emails from the user → THEN the assistant may call find_user_id_by_email on each candidate email until one returns a user_id.
- IF a tool call that expects an order_id is made using a non-order identifier and the tool returns Error: Order not found → THEN the assistant may search the user's known orders (from get_user_details(user.orders)) by calling get_order_details on those ids to locate which order contains the referenced item_id, and then retry the action with that order_id prefixed by "#W..." (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory).
- IF the assistant must compute totals for a product across the user's orders (for example "total price for all the grills I’ve bought") → THEN call get_order_details on each order in get_user_details(user.orders), find items whose product_id matches the requested product, sum their item.price values, and present the sum to the user. (Observed Behavior — supported by Conversation 5).
- IF modify_pending_order_items returns an Error referencing malformed item_ids or unknown item string → THEN (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory) attempt a retry after cleaning the item_ids (remove UI text/prefixes) and call modify_pending_order_items again.
- IF a variant selection or price computation is presented to the user and the user confirms → THEN perform the corresponding state-changing tool call immediately without further confirmation.

6. State-Dependent Rules
- IF order.status == "pending" → THEN:
  - use modify_pending_order_items to change items for that order,
  - use modify_pending_order_address to change the shipping address for that order,
  - call modify_pending_order_items / modify_pending_order_address separately for each pending order the user requested to update.
  - IF the user requests cancellation of only one item in a pending order that contains multiple items → THEN inform the user that cancelling only part of a pending order is not allowed, offer the option to return those items after delivery, and (if the user asked for the refund amount) compute and present the sum of the prices of the items that match the described subset so the user knows the expected refund total. (Observed Behavior — supported by Conversation 5.)
- IF order.status == "processed" OR order.status == "cancelled" AND the user requests reinstatement of that order → THEN transfer the user to a human agent. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory in all cases.)
- IF order.status == "processed" or order.status == "delivered" (non-pending) AND the user requests returns → THEN use return_delivered_order_items for those delivered orders (do not use modify_pending_order_items).
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND the user requests exchanges → THEN use exchange_delivered_order_items for those delivered orders.
- IF the user requests a refund to the same payment method used in the original payment_history entry for that order → THEN the assistant uses that payment_method_id in the return/exchange call and informs the user that refund is to that original payment.
- IF original payment method is a gift_card → THEN refunds must be to that gift_card or another existing gift_card on the user's account.
- IF new_item_price > old_item_price during modify_pending_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items → THEN a payment transaction for the difference will be recorded to payment_method_id.
- IF new_item_price < old_item_price during modify_pending_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items → THEN a refund transaction for the difference will be recorded to payment_method_id.
- IF multiple pending orders contain items of the same product and the user asks to update "all pending X orders" → THEN locate all orders with status "pending" that include product_id for X by calling get_order_details on user.orders and apply modify_pending_order_items to each pending order separately.
- IF the assistant needs to compute price differences or refunds for exchanges/cheapest selections or batch modifications → THEN:
  - call list_all_product_types to locate product_id (if product name was given),
  - call get_product_details(product_id) to obtain variant prices and availability,
  - compute the sum of selected variant prices (use variants with available==true) and compare to the sum of old item prices,
  - present the computed price/payment or refund amount to the user and ask for confirmation before executing the action.
- IF the assistant is changing an order address to one taken from another order → THEN call get_order_details on the referenced order id and extract its address fields to pass to modify_pending_order_address or modify_user_address.
- IF the user requests a refund to a payment method that is not allowed by the order's original payment rules (for example: user asks refund to credit card but order.payment_history payment_method_id source == "gift_card") → THEN inform the user that refund cannot go to the requested payment method and must go to the original allowed method (gift card), offer to proceed with the allowed method, and if the user agrees proceed to call return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items with the gift_card id. (Supported by Conversation 3.)

7. Error Handling Rules (concise)
- IF get_order_details returns Error: Order not found → THEN ask the user to re-check the order id and retry the get_order_details call when the user supplies a corrected id.
- IF find_user_id_by_name_zip returns Error: User not found → THEN ask for email and call find_user_id_by_email(email).
- IF modify_user_address returns Error: User not found (e.g., due to malformed user_id) → THEN retry modify_user_address with the user_id exactly as returned by get_user_details.
- IF return_delivered_order_items or other action returns Error: Payment method not found → THEN retry with a valid payment_method_id from get_user_details(payment_methods) (retry once).
- IF a tool call that uses a payment_method_id returns Error: Payment method not found due to malformed id in the request → THEN reformat the request and retry using the valid id from get_user_details. (Observed Behavior — seen.)
- IF modify_pending_order_address returns Error: unexpected keyword argument 'ZIP' (or other parameter-key errors) → THEN retry the call with parameter name 'zip' (lowercase).
- IF get_order_details was called with "#<id>" and returned Error: Order not found → THEN assistant may retry with "#W<id>" (observed once). (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory.)
- IF find_user_id_by_name_zip fails and the user supplies an alternative zip or corrected name → THEN retry find_user_id_by_name_zip with the corrected values until a user_id is returned or the user supplies an email. (Observed Behavior — used as retry loop.)
- IF modify_pending_order_items returns Error: item not found due to malformed item_id string → THEN (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory) attempt cleaning of supplied item_ids (strip UI text/prefixes) and retry the modify_pending_order_items call.

8. Transfer / Human Escalation Rules
- IF the user explicitly demands a human agent (phrases like "Get me a real person now" or "###TRANSFER###") → THEN transfer the user to a human agent immediately.
- IF the user requests an action that the assistant refuses because it is not allowed (for example, refund to an arbitrary other card when the allowed options are original payment or gift card) AND the user requests escalation → THEN offer and perform transfer to a human agent. (Observed Behavior — supported by trajectories.)
- IF the user requests a refund to a payment method different from the order's original payment_history payment method AND that is not allowed by the system (e.g., delivered order where refund must go to original) → THEN inform the user that refunds for delivered orders must go to the original payment method (or gift card constraints where applicable) and offer transfer to a human agent if the user asks for escalation. (Observed Behavior — supported by Conversation 3.)

Observed Behaviors (not proven mandatory) — short list
- Assistant sometimes supplied default cancellation reason "no longer needed" when cancelling pending orders without explicit reason. (seen)
- Assistant may provide tracking numbers from order.fulfillments when mentioning cancelled/returned orders. (seen)
- Assistant may retry get_order_details with a "#W..." prefixed id if "#<id>" fails. (observed once)
- Assistant may propose alternative variants when a requested exact-attribute variant is not available — presents alternatives and asks whether user wants them. (observed)
- Assistant may retry a name-based find with a corrected spelling/zip before asking for email if the initial find_user_id_by_name_zip returns Error. (observed)
- Assistant may search get_user_details(user.orders) and call get_order_details on those ids to locate an order containing a referenced item_id, and then retry actions with the located "#W..." order id. (observed once)
- Assistant may prompt for first_name/last_name/zip for non-account product inquiries. (observed)
- Assistant retried modify_pending_order_items after an Error: item not found by cleaning the supplied item_id string (removing UI prefixes). (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

End of updated policy.
